User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the TestAI application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a QA project within the TestAI application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the project entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was created.

TestCase.title: TestCase
TestCase.description: Represents a test case within a project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the test case entity.

module.description: Module or feature the test case belongs to.

priority.description: Priority of the test case (Low, Medium, High, Critical).

severity.description: Severity of the potential defect if the test fails.

preconditions.description: Preconditions required to execute the test case.

testSteps.description: Step-by-step instructions for executing the test case.

expectedResult.description: Expected outcome of the test case execution.

tags.description: References to Tags. (Relationship: Tag N:N TestCase)

automationFeasibility.description: Indicates whether the test case is suitable for automation.

automationPriority.description: Priority for automating the test case.

subModule.description: The sub module that this test case is testing.

team.description: The specific team in charge of this test case.

sprint.description: The sprint this test case belongs to.

release.description: The release this test case belongs to.

testData.description: Specific data related to this test case.

createdBy.description: The user that created this test case.

createdDate.description: The date this test case was created.

status.description: The approval status of the test case.

TestExecution.title: TestExecution
TestExecution.description: Represents the execution of a test case.

id.description: Unique identifier for the test execution entity.

testerName.description: Name of the tester who executed the test case.

executionDate.description: Date when the test case was executed.

environment.description: Environment in which the test case was executed.

buildVersion.description: Build version of the application being tested.

resultStatus.description: Result status of the test execution (Passed, Failed, Deferred, etc.).

comments.description: Comments or notes about the test execution.

AutomationScript.title: AutomationScript
AutomationScript.description: Represents an automation script generated for a test case.

id.description: Unique identifier for the automation script entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the automation script was generated.

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Represents a tag or label that can be applied to test cases.

id.description: Unique identifier for the tag entity.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  User's ID is derived from Firebase Authentication.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores projects owned by a specific user. Authorization is based on path ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project.

definition.description: Stores test cases associated with a specific project. Authorization is based on project ownership, derived from the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project containing the test case.

params.name: testCaseId
params.description: The unique ID of the test case.

definition.description: Stores test executions associated with a specific project.  Authorization is based on project ownership, derived from the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project containing the test execution.

params.name: testExecutionId
params.description: The unique ID of the test execution.

definition.description: Stores automation scripts associated with a specific project. Authorization is based on project ownership, derived from the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project containing the automation script.

params.name: automationScriptId
params.description: The unique ID of the automation script.

definition.description: Stores global tags that can be applied to test cases.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique ID of the tag.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It leverages path-based ownership for user-related data and employs denormalization to avoid `get()` calls in security rules, thus ensuring atomic operations and simplifying debugging. The structure supports secure `list` operations through structural segregation, maintaining a homogeneous security posture within each collection.

Specifically, the `/users/{userId}` collection stores user profiles. Projects are stored in `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}`, establishing clear ownership and eliminating the need to check user roles during project access. Test cases, test executions, and automation scripts follow a similar pattern, residing under their respective projects (`/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/testCases/{testCaseId}`, `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/testExecutions/{testExecutionId}`, and `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/automationScripts/{automationScriptId}`), thereby ensuring that access to these resources is implicitly controlled by project ownership. Tags are stored globally under `/tags/{tagId}` since they are not user-specific and are intended to be shared across the application.

This design ensures Authorization Independence by nesting project-related data under the user's path, inherently linking project ownership to the user's ID.  QAPs are supported through segregation: the path structure itself enforces that only the owner can list projects they own, test cases they own, etc. There are no `get()` calls to a parent document needed to determine access; the path dictates authorization. Global tags don't require user-specific authorization, so are stored in a separate collection.

This structure avoids complex security rules and ensures data integrity by enforcing clear ownership and consistent access patterns.
